text: The world around me feels oddly silent—a corridor bathed in harsh artificial light, tilted as if the ground beneath me has shifted. My mind wonders about the emptiness; the glass doors reflect not just the dim surroundings, but inner questions about presence and absence. The space is sterile, bordering on desolate, with a sense of waiting—waiting for movement, for purpose, for someone else to arrive. A feeling of detachment seeps in, as if I'm an observer isolated from the usual rhythm of life.
